member.name: Patrick Hurley
member.synopsis: Patrick Hurley is the Labour MP for Southport , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Jobcentre Plus
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what estimate his Department has made of the number of people who want to work but are not currently engaged with Jobcentre Plus services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of our plans to Get Britain Working and create a new Jobs and Careers Service, we are making employment and careers support available to everyone, including those who are not currently engaged with Jobcentre Plus services. This includes our recent...

writtenQuestions.heading: Poverty
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment he has made of the effectiveness of cross-government approaches to tackling poverty in areas experiencing deprivation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The drivers of poverty span employment, social security, housing, education, childcare, health and the cost of living. Cross-government action is therefore essential to effectively tackle poverty. That is why the Child Poverty Strategy was developed by a ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment and Poverty: Voluntary Organisations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment his Department has made of the adequacy of the contribution of local voluntary, community and social enterprise organisations to reducing poverty and supporting employment outcomes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is clear that tackling poverty and improving employment outcomes requires partnership working and that government cannot achieve this alone. The voluntary, community and social enterprise (VCSE) sector is the bedrock of our communities, pro...

writtenQuestions.heading: Packaging: Recycling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, with reference to the Answer of 29 June 2026 to Question 12585 on Packaging: Recycling, whether the ongoing review of the wider impacts of Extended Producer Responsibility for packagin
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under the scheme, obligations are based on packaging placed on the market and statutory thresholds, rather than business model. Producers are exempt from disposal fee and recycling obligations where they have annual turnover below £2 million and place les...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Infrastructure: Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what discussions he has had with the Secretary of State for Education on the role of community infrastructure in improving educational attainment in disadvantaged communities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State engages regularly with ministerial colleagues on a range of matters. This includes the flagship £5.8 billion Pride in Place Programme which is targeting places experiencing the highest levels of deprivation, providing flexible fundi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: NATO
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to strengthen the UK’s relationship with NATO allies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NATO is the cornerstone of our security, and we are strengthening our relationships with Allies every day.Together we support Ukraine, develop new capabilities, and deepen defence partnerships, with new bilateral agreements already signed with France, Ger...

writtenQuestions.heading: Youth Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what assessment she has made of the impact of levels of (a) youth services and (b) wider community infrastructure on educational outcomes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government published Youth Matters: Your National Youth Strategy in December, following extensive research through qualitative data collection and wider evidence collation, and direct engagement with young people to understand their needs. It sets ou...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Education: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the potential implications for her policies of the findings of the Inquiry into White Working Class Educational Outcomes, published in June 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department welcomes the findings of the Inquiry and, as stated in the ‘Every Child Achieving and Thriving’ White Paper, intends to build on its work to develop better insights into issues specific to white working-class children, families and their re...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pupils: Absenteeism
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to reduce rates of persistent and severe absence among pupils from disadvantaged backgrounds in coastal towns.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Absence is a key barrier to opportunity. For children to achieve and thrive they need to be in school. The department is committed to improving the attendance of all pupils, regardless of background and geographical location. Thanks to the efforts of the ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Land: Valuation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what discussions she has had with mayoral combined authorities on the use of land value capture mechanisms to support the financing of major transport infrastructure projects.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to delivering strong, secure and sustainable growth to lift living standards in every community, town and city region, backing local leaders as the best people to deliver growth in their area. This includes improving the Busine...

writtenQuestions.heading: Transport: Taxation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of extending tax increment financing mechanisms to support the delivery of major transport infrastructure projects.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to delivering strong, secure and sustainable growth to lift living standards in every community, town and city region, backing local leaders as the best people to deliver growth in their area. This includes improving the Busine...

writtenQuestions.heading: Infrastructure: Taxation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether she plans to allow mayoral combined authorities to retain a proportion of additional tax revenues generated by major infrastructure investments in their areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: At her Mais Lecture, the Chancellor committed to work with mayors, businesses and other stakeholders to develop a fiscal devolution roadmap, to be published at the Budget, setting out plans to give regional leaders control over the allocation of a share o...

writtenQuestions.heading: Development Corporations: Borrowing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of permitting development corporations to borrow for regeneration projects outside the Government's fiscal rules on economic growth.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the important role that development corporations can play in unlocking large-scale regeneration andsupporting economic growth, including through coordinating investment, land assembly, and accelerating delivery. Development corpo...

writtenQuestions.heading: Coastal Areas: Regeneration
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of fiscal arrangements in supporting long-term regeneration in coastal communities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to working in partnership with the nation’s coastal and rural communities, including Southport, so that they can kickstart growth in their area and make everyone better off.Many coastal communities sit within mayoral combined a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Lipoedema
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of lipoedema on commonly associated co-morbidities, including hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not hold centrally collected data on the average time taken to diagnose lipoedema in England. Diagnosis is typically made clinically in primary or specialist care, and timelines can vary depending on individual presentation, awareness ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Vocational Education: Coastal Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of the availability of (a) apprenticeships and (b) technical training opportunities for young people in coastal communities.

writtenQuestions.heading: Antisemitism: Hate Crime
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of trends in the level of regional variation in arrests and prosecutions relating to antisemitic hate crime offences.
